codeReview.summary: The implementation comprehensively covers the e-text backend foundations with proper data modeling, persistence layer, and API handlers. The worker added 9 new files with ~3,646 lines of code including 37 test cases. All validators (go test, go vet, go build) pass. The implementation correctly enforces owner-scoping, implements LCS-based diff, revision-chain blame, and auth-gated endpoints.

issues.description: Documentation mentions 'Dolt-backed' in package comment but implementation uses SQLite. The code is well-designed for future Dolt migration (per-user workspace databases, schema design), but the comment should clarify this is SQLite with migration path to Dolt.

The LCS diff implementation builds a full DP table which has O(m*n) space complexity. For large documents, this could be memory-intensive. Consider documenting this limitation or implementing Hirschberg's algorithm for large inputs (comment acknowledges this but uses full table for simplicity).

Blame algorithm has a TODO comment noting the approach is 'approximate but sufficient for section-level blame'. The implementation could be more precise by properly mapping diff sections to line ranges, though current approach works for typical use cases.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The .factory/services.yaml file does not include e-text endpoints for testing. The e-text API handlers at /api/etext/* are not documented in the services file, which could make manual testing/verification harder for future workers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: services.yaml only lists auth, proxy, vmctl, gateway, sandbox, and frontend services. The new e-text endpoints (POST /api/etext/documents, GET /api/etext/diff, etc.) are not documented there.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The .factory/library/etext-store-foundation.md was created by the worker to document the e-text feature, but this pattern of documenting features in library markdown files is not standardized. Consider whether this should be merged into a broader architecture document or kept as a feature-specific reference.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: internal/library/etext-store-foundation.md exists with 107 lines documenting data model, store layer, API endpoints, diff/blame algorithms, and routing.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The e-text implementation follows existing codebase patterns well: SQLite with WAL mode, SetMaxOpenConns(1), same-origin auth with X-Authenticated-User header, owner-scoped queries, and proper error handling. The worker correctly matched existing patterns without explicit documentation in AGENTS.md.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Store uses modernc.org/sqlite with PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL, follows pattern from auth/runtime stores. API handlers use authenticateUser() helper matching existing auth patterns.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The go-worker skill was followed correctly. The worker ran init.sh, read all required documents (mission.md, validation-contract.md, AGENTS.md, architecture.md), and followed the prescribed procedure including baseline tests before implementation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: read mission.md, validation-contract.md, features.json, AGENTS.md, services.yaml, architecture.md, ran go test ./... -count=1 -p 4 as baseline, then proceeded with implementation.

summary: The etext-store-history-diff-blame-foundation feature is fully implemented and passes review. The worker delivered: (1) Complete types package with Document, Revision, AuthorKind, Citation, DiffResult, BlameResult, HistoryEntry types; (2) Store layer with document/revision CRUD, owner-scoped queries, LCS-based diff, revision-chain blame; (3) API handlers for all endpoints with auth gating; (4) 37 test cases covering store, types, and API; (5) Library documentation. All validators pass. Minor non-blocking observations: SQLite is used (not Dolt, but migration-ready), LCS memory usage for large docs, blame precision could be improved. No blocking issues. Shared state gaps: e-text endpoints not in services.yaml.
